steps.title: Read histogram frequencies and locate median positions
steps.content: From the histogram, the original 11 years distribute as: 2 in [40, 45), 3 in [45, 50), 3 in [50, 55), 1 in [55, 60), and 2 in [75, 80). The original median is the 6th value (middle of 11 ordered values). Cumulative count: positions 1–2 in [40, 45), positions 3–5 in [45, 50), positions 6–8 in [50, 55), position 9 in [55, 60), positions 10–11 in [75, 80). The original median is the 6th value, located in [50, 55).

steps.title: Statement I: Does removing 76.2°F decrease the mean?
steps.content: For the mean to decrease when a value is removed, that value must be greater than the current mean. The data clusters in [40, 60), with only 2 values in [75, 80). The mean is approximately $\frac{2(42.5) + 3(47.5) + 3(52.5) + 1(57.5) + 2(76.5)}{11} \approx \frac{595}{11} \approx 54°F$. Since 76.2°F $\gg$ 54°F, removing it makes the numerator smaller while the denominator shrinks from 11 to 10, thus the new mean $= \frac{\text{old sum} - 76.2}{10} <$ original mean. Statement I is TRUE.

steps.title: Statement II: Does removing 76.2°F decrease the median?
steps.content:
After removing one value from [75, 80), the new data has 10 values. The new median is the average of the 5th and 6th values. The cumulative counts remain: positions 1–2 in [40, 45), positions 3–5 in [45, 50), positions 6–8 in [50, 55), position 9 in [55, 60), position 10 in [75, 80). The 5th value is in [45, 50) and the 6th value is in [50, 55). Therefore:
Original median = 6th value only (a value in [50, 55))
New median = $\frac{\text{5th value} + \text{6th value}}{2}$ = $\frac{\text{(value in [45,50)} + \text{value in [50,55))}}{2}$
Since the 5th value is below 50 and the 6th value is at or above 50, their average is strictly less than the 6th value alone. Statement II is TRUE.

steps.title: Both statements are true
steps.content: Since removing 76.2°F (which lies above the mean and far above the median) necessarily decreases the mean, and changes the median from a single value in [50, 55) to the average of a value below 50 and a value in [50, 55), both statements I and II must be true. That matches choice C — C is correct.
